description.long: A practical introduction to Figma's most powerful responsive tools. You will learn how to structure a button using Auto Layout to handle padding and alignment, and apply Constraints to ensure it adapts perfectly to any screen width.

steps.title: Create the label
steps.description: Press T, click anywhere on the canvas, and type Get Started.
steps.tip: Always start with the text content before drawing a rectangle.

steps.title: Style the typography
steps.description:
With the text selected, look at the Text section in the right sidebar.
Set the Font to Inter, Weight to Medium, and Size to 24px.

steps.title: Add Auto Layout
steps.description: Press <kbd>Shift + A</kbd> to wrap the text in an Auto Layout frame. In the Layers panel (left), rename this frame to 'Button'.
steps.tip: Auto Layout allows the button to grow automatically if you change the text later.

steps.title: Adjust spacing and alignment
steps.description:
Go to the Auto Layout section in the right sidebar:
  • Set Vertical Padding to 16px.
  • Click the Center Icon in the alignment grid (middle dot).
  • Ensure Vertical Resizing is set to Hug.
steps.tip: Setting resizing to 'Hug' keeps the button container tight to the text size.

steps.title: Style the Button
steps.description:
Let's make it pop. In the right sidebar:
  • Fill: Change 'Solid' to 'Linear'. Set top to #AFD7FE and bottom to #8AC5FE.
  • Stroke: Click +. Set color to #FFFFFF (White) at 20% opacity. Set position to Inside.
  • Corner Radius: Set it to 16.

steps.title: Create the Phone Screen
steps.description: Press F to open the Frame tool. In the right sidebar, select iPhone 14/15 Pro (or any 393px width screen). Rename the frame to 'Screen'.

steps.title: Place the button
steps.description: Drag and drop your 'Button' inside the 'Screen' frame.
steps.tip: Important: Look at the Layers panel on the left. Ensure 'Button' is nested INSIDE 'Screen'.

steps.title: Set the Width
steps.description: We want a 20px margin on each side. Select the button. In the W (Width) field in the right sidebar, type: 393-40 and press Enter.
steps.tip: Figma calculates the math automatically to give you the perfect width (353px).

steps.title: Position the Button
steps.description:
Let's place it perfectly at the bottom.
  • Select the button and press Alt + H (Center horizontally).
  • Press Alt + S (Align bottom).
  • Hold Shift and press the Up Arrow key twice.
steps.tip: Holding Shift moves objects by 10px. Pressing it twice creates a 20px bottom margin.

steps.title: Set Constraints
steps.description:
This is the magic step. Go to the Constraints tab (Right sidebar, top):
  • Set Horizontal to Left and Right.
  • Set Vertical to Bottom.
steps.tip: Result: The button will now stretch if the screen gets wider, and stick to the bottom if the screen gets taller.

steps.title: Test your work
steps.description: Select the 'Screen' frame. Drag the right edge to make it wider or narrower. The button should stretch and shrink automatically!
